exemplar.title: How does the viscosity of the fluid affect the mass and volumetric flow of an incompressible fluid in a vortex?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the viscosity of the fluid affect the mass and volumetric flow of an incompressible fluid in a vortex?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is sharply formulated and sustained throughout the investigation
Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with clear purpose and focus
Methodology is complete with an appropriate range of sources/methods and clear evidence of effective selection
weaknesses: Justification for the choice of 1500 rpm is not provided
Figure 1 caption could better explain its relevance to experimental design and viscosity predictions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work firmly in the top band for focus and method. The research question is crystal-clear, the introduction and subsequent sections continually re-engage with it, and the methodology is robust and replicable. Minor omissions (rpm justification, expanded figure captions) prevent a theoretical perfect, but the sustained clarity and completeness justify the maximum 6 marks.

strengths: Terminology and concepts (vorticity, circulation, boundary layer, flow rates) are used accurately and consistently
Excellent command of fluid-dynamics theory is applied coherently to interpret experimental outcomes
weaknesses: Background discussion would benefit from deeper quantitative relationships
Some source citations are incomplete or non-scholarly, limiting the breadth of academic rigour
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates the highest level of subject knowledge: equations and units are handled precisely, and advanced fluid-dynamics concepts underpin the analysis. Although fuller quantitative detail and more rigorous sourcing would perfect this strand, the consistent, coherent application of terminology and theory merits the top score of 6.

strengths: Data analysis is systematic and clearly linked to the research question, with uncertainty propagation and RÂ² interpretation
Arguments are well structured, moving logically from theory to evidence
Limitations are identified and realistic improvements proposed
weaknesses: Advanced statistical techniques (confidence intervals, residual diagnostics) are not employed
Quantitative estimation of error impacts (e.g., boundary-layer thickness, Reynolds number) is missing
Counter-argument section addresses only one objection and could be expanded
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis consistently focuses on the research question, and the discussion develops a coherent, evidence-based argument. Critical evaluation of errors and proposal of improvements demonstrate good critical thinking. The absence of more sophisticated statistical depth and quantitative error estimates keeps the work in the mid-high of the 7â9 band, supporting a score of 8.

strengths: Report structure and formatting follow IB conventions impeccably, with clear headings, numbered sections, and a comprehensive table of contents
Layout is tidy, figures and tables are well placed and annotated
Bibliography and referencing conventions are consistently applied
weaknesses: A few figures lack scale bars or optimal resolution
Colour-coding in graphs (red/green) may present accessibility issues
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fully meets the highest band descriptors. The documentâs structure, formatting, and visual layout support seamless reader navigation. Minor accessibility considerations do not detract from the overall clarity, justifying the maximum 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Terminology such as âvorticity,â âcirculation,â âmass flow rate,â âvolumetric flow rate,â and âboundary layerâ is used accurately and consistently.  Equations are correctly formatted and referenced, and units (Pa s, kg sâ»Â¹, mÂ³ sâ»Â¹) are mostly presented with appropriate prefixes.

Minor lapsesâoccasional missing multiplication dots or inconsistent significant-figure usageâprevent a perfect performance, but the precision and consistency clearly exceed the expectations for the 3â4 band.  A strong 5 is therefore appropriate.

The narrative moves logically from theory to data to interpretation.  Claims are generally supported by evidence, and the student anticipates counter-arguments (e.g. constant stirrer power) and addresses them with reasoned refutations.  Graphs are integrated into the discussion, reinforcing the argument that viscosity inversely affects flow.

Some leapsâsuch as the precise cause of the logarithmic plateauâare asserted without rigorous quantitative modelling, and the argument could be strengthened by deeper comparison with literature.  This places the work at the lower end of the 7-9 band (score 7).

The methodology is carefully designed and replicable: apparatus, controlled/independent/dependent variables, and a step-by-step procedure are all clearly tabulated or enumerated.  The use of a logarithmic blending equation, repeated trials, uncertainty treatment, and safety considerations show informed choices.

Minor omissionsâsuch as fuller justification for the choice of 1500 rpm, calibration details for the modified beaker/valve assembly, and a more rigorous plan for statistical testingâprevent it from being faultless.  Nevertheless, the range and depth of methods comfortably satisfy the expectations of the top band, albeit nearer the lower end (score 5 rather than 6).

The student demonstrates **excellent command of fluid-dynamics concepts** (NavierâStokes momentum conservation, vorticity, boundary-layer behaviour) and applies them appropriately to interpret experimental outcomes.  Calculations of blended viscosity, propagation of percentage uncertainties, and linkage between theoretical inverses (Î¼ â 1/u) and observed trends reveal a coherent and largely accurate understanding.

A few statements are slightly over-generalised (e.g. plateau explanation lacks quantitative backing) and some sources are non-scholarly, but these issues are minor in the overall context of strong subject mastery.  Hence a high score within the 5-6 band is justified.

The student identifies systematic and random errors, explains their physical origins, and proposes realistic improvements (e.g. factory-made beaker with tangential outlet, electronic valve, multiple operators).  The discussion of boundary-layer limitations shows an awareness of how design choices affect validity.

However, evaluation is uneven: quantitative impact of errors is not estimated, and alternative methods (e.g. PIV flow visualisation, statistical controls) are not explored.  The work therefore meets the âgood evaluation with some critical analysisâ standard at the top of the 4-6 band (score 6).

The studentâs topicâinvestigating how viscosity influences mass and volumetric flow in a vortexâis stated explicitly in the title, reiterated in the introduction, and kept in focus throughout the paper.  The introduction contextualises vortices in both natural and engineered settings, explains why the relationship is worth studying, and connects directly to the subsequent literature review, methodology, and analysis. 

Explanations are concise yet detailed, allowing a non-specialist reader to grasp the purpose and scope of the work without ambiguity.  Headings, signposting phrases (e.g. âIn this investigationâ¦,â âTherefore, this study aimsâ¦â), and consistent linkage to the research question ensure that the reader never loses sight of the central theme.  Overall, communication is clear, purposeful, and sustained, fully matching the descriptors for the top band.

Figures are legible, appropriately annotated, and placed close to their discussion.  Tables use clear headings and uncertainty notation.  The layout (margins, fonts, spacing) is tidy and consistent.

A small number of images lack scale bars or have moderate resolution, and colour choices (red/green trend lines) could challenge colour-blind readers.  These minor shortcomings keep the strand slightly below perfect, justifying a band 3-4 placement with a score of 3.

The research question is sharply formulated (âHow does the fluidâs viscosity affect an incompressible fluidâs mass and volumetric flow in a vortex?â).  It is specific, measurable (flow rate for defined volumes), and tightly bounded to a single, manipulable independent variable (viscosity achieved via glycerinâwater mixtures).  

Throughout the essay the student continually re-engages with this question: the literature review justifies its significance, the data analysis is organised around answering it, and the conclusion restates the answer while acknowledging limitations.  The clarity and sustained focus meet the highest expectations of the 5â6 band.

Data treatment is systematic: three trials per condition, propagation of instrumental uncertainties, calculation of averages, and graphical analysis with RÂ² values.  Trends are interpreted in light of theoretical models, and limitations such as boundary-layer effects are discussed, showing **clear, consistent linkage to the research question**.

Advanced statistical depth (confidence intervals, residual diagnostics) is limited, so the work does not fully reach the sophistication of the 10-12 band.  Nonetheless, analysis quality is solidly within the 7-9 descriptor; a mid-band score of 8 is warranted.

The report follows IB EE conventions impeccably: title page with word count, abstract/introductory elements, numbered sections, contents page, correctly sequenced headings, and bibliography.  Equations, figures, and tables are consistently numbered and referenced in the text.  Pagination and overall formatting support fluent reading.

Minor issues (occasional repetitive figure captions, a few long URLs not shortened) remain, yet they do not detract from overall clarity.  This meets the highest band for structure and formatting (score 4).

annotations.comment: The historical examples are engaging but lack citation of primary scholarly sources; consider replacing general website references with peer-reviewed articles to strengthen credibility.
The title is clearly stated at the start of the report, providing immediate context for the reader and fulfilling the formatting requirement.
Variable identification is thorough, but the controller for stirrer speed at 1500 rpm lacks justification; include rationale for this specific choice.
The detailed table of contents follows conventional structure, facilitating navigation and reflecting effective presentation of material.
The research question is stated clearly and precisely, guiding the investigation with strong focus throughout the introduction.
The breakdown of factors affecting viscosity and the use of logarithmic blending is well-justified, indicating a methodically chosen and appropriate methodology.
Figure 4âs red/green trend lines could present accessibility issues for colour-blind readers; consider symbols or line styles in addition to colour.
Background discussion correctly describes vortex dissipation but would benefit from quantitative relationships or equations to deepen subject knowledge application.
The controlled variables table is comprehensive and well-structured, enhancing clarity of experimental controls.
The inclusion of a precise word count at the beginning of the document meets IB formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to the maximum limit constraint.
The conclusion restates the research question and succinctly links findings to theoretical expectations, demonstrating strong focus and method alignment.
Concluding reference to Ofuchi et al. is appropriate, but including more peer-reviewed articles would improve the breadth of scholarly source application.
Page numbers are consistently present, supporting navigability and fulfilling basic formatting conventions.
Some source citations (e.g., B Giaiotti & Stel) are incomplete; include full publication details to demonstrate rigorous use of terminology and concepts.
Figure 1 is informative, but the caption could be expanded to explain its direct relevance to experimental design and viscosity predictions.
Equation [1] is correctly presented and all variables are defined, demonstrating accurate use of fundamental fluid dynamics concepts.
Precise physical properties for glycerin and water are provided with appropriate units, reflecting solid subject knowledge application.
Annotated photograph of experimental setup is clear and labeled effectively, supporting reproducibility of the methodology.
Processed data table correctly propagates uncertainties, but inconsistent significant-figure usage undermines precision; standardize to two significant figures.
Uncertainty calculations are explicitly shown, reflecting critical analysis of experimental precision and error propagation.
The qualitative explanation of boundary-layer effects is insightful, but would be strengthened by quantitative estimation of layer thickness or Reynolds number.
Linking the plateau to stationary fluid near the beaker wall demonstrates mature argument development and insight into flow dynamics.
Figure 5 integrates a data table within the image container, which interrupts flow; separate the table from the graph for cleaner layout.
The counter-argument section addresses one objection; consider adding further objections (e.g., temperature fluctuations) with refutations to deepen critical thinking.
Limitations section identifies key errors but could include advanced alternatives (e.g., PIV flow visualization) to enhance critical evaluation of methodology.
References list contains numerous web and video sources; prioritize peer-reviewed journals and standardize reference formatting to enhance presentation.
